name: Israel Crosby
abstract: Israel Crosby (January 19, 1919 – August 11, 1962) was an American jazz double-bassist born in Chicago, Illinois, United States. One of the finest to emerge during the 1930s, he was also a member of the Ahmad Jamal trio for most of 1954 to 1962.

concerts.label: Jazz Concert by Benny Goodman and Louis Armstrong
Midnight Performance: Jazz Concert by Benny Goodman and Louis Armstrong
Dakota Staton, Singer, the Ahmad Jamal Trio, and the Ted Heath Orchestra
Midnight Performance: Dakota Staton, Singer, the Ahmad Jamal Trio, and the Ted Heath Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part II
about_quotes.evidence: I had this gig at the Beehive Lounge in Chicago, where I was with the house band with Israel Crosby on bass
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part II interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 18, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: I have never worked with any bass player that I enjoyed working with any more than Israel Crosby.

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: I later joined Fletcher at the Grant Terrace when our band broke up. I joined Fletcher; I took Israel Crosby with me.

about_quotes.speaker: Norman Simmons
about_quotes.evidence: I had played with Israel Crosby at the Bee Hive, when I first went to the Bee Hive to play, Israel was there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Norman Simmons interviewed by Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 30, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: we had a fellow named Pete Suggs on drums, Israel Crosby on bass and Larry Lucie on guitar.

about_quotes.speaker: Rashied Ali and Henry Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: There was bass players like Oscar Pettiford and Ray Brown and Percy Heath and bass players like that, Israel Crosby and two more, you know, very good bass players, technically.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rashied Ali and Henry Grimes interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, January 30, 2009
